title: Crispy Ethiopian Chicken Burger
description: A spicy, crispy Ethiopian chicken burger that delivers a salty, molten bite with a double-fry crunch, perfect for a quick weeknight meal.
instructions: In a bowl, combine chicken, berbere, salt, pepper, garlic powder, and half the olive oil; mash until well mixed.
Mix egg and remaining olive oil; fold into chicken mixture; then stir in almond flour until thickened.
Form the mixture into 4 equal patties, about 2 cm thick, and place on a parchment-lined tray.
Bake the patties for 15 minutes until firm but still slightly pink inside.
Heat 3 cups of oil in a deep pan to 170°C (340°F); once hot, reduce heat to 150°C (300°F).
Fry the patty halves for 3 minutes each, then transfer to a paper towel to drain.
Increase the oil temperature to 190°C (375°F) and fry each patty again for 2 minutes until golden and crispy.
While the second fry finishes, place the lettuce leaves on plates, top with basil, then add fried patty.
Drizzle hot sauce over the patty and serve immediately.
